title: centaurea phrygia subsp. phrygia
intro: Centaurea phrygia subsp. phrygia, also known as Phrygian cornflower, is a species of flowering plant in the daisy family. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects. The health benefits of this plant are attributed to its ability to soothe digestive issues, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory problems. The plant can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or infusion for medicinal use, with the leaves and flowers being the most commonly used parts.
